Football.title: Mikel Arteta's intriguing Arsenal comparison to Arsene Wenger's title winners at same stage
Football.content:
Mikel Arteta's intriguing Arsenal comparison to Arsene Wenger's title winners at same stage

Mikel Arteta is looking to win Arsenal their first Premier League title since 2004 with the Gunners in a strong position

View 3 Images Mikel Arteta is hoping to lead Arsenal to the title as Arsene Wenger did (Image: Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

Arsenal claimed three Premier League titles under legendary manager Arsene Wenger, with the Frenchman's inaugural triumph arriving in 1998. Following championship victories in 1989 and 1991, Wenger re-established the Gunners' supremacy by dethroning Manchester United in an epic title battle.

‌



Fast forward to today and Mikel Arteta is currently aiming to etch his name into Arsenal's prestigious legacy by delivering the north London outfit their first championship since 2004.

‌



Yet, whilst the 1998 double-winning squad are justifiably celebrated for their achievements, Arteta's team are genuinely in a superior position compared to Wenger's legendary side at the equivalent point of the campaign.

‌



Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

The present Arsenal squad sit five points ahead of second-placed Manchester City at the top of the table. Though the Citizens control their fate with Pep Guardiola's outfit possessing a match in hand and still needing to face Arsenal.

As of March 2, 1998, Arsenal occupied second place in the division, sitting nine points behind United, having recently drawn 0-0 with West Ham. Though Arsenal held three matches in hand over Sir Alex Ferguson's outfit.

‌



How many wins Arsenal and Man City need to secure Premier League title READ MORE :

Arsene Wenger's radical offside rule change takes next step despite UEFA rejection READ MORE :

What subsequently unfolded was nothing short of legendary, as Arsenal embarked on a remarkable 10-match winning streak to secure the championship. Indeed, following a 3-1 defeat to Blackburn Rovers in mid-December 1997, the Gunners claimed 17 victories from 19 matches, with three draws.

Their subsequent losses were inflicted by Liverpool and Aston Villa in their last two matches of the season, after Wenger's squad had already clinched the title. Arsenal are now a mere nine games away from immortality following their 2-1 victory over Chelsea at The Emirates Stadium on Sunday.

However, fans will be pondering whether Arteta's team possess the same mindset as Wenger's 1998 side: one capable of driving them to an astonishing winning streak to secure the championship.

‌



View 3 Images Arsenal manager Arsene Wenger and club captain Tony Adams hold the Premier League trophy after the match against Everton at Highbury in 1998 (Image: Ben Radford /Allsport)

At this juncture, City can amass a maximum of 89 points, assuming they triumph over the Gunners at the Etihad Stadium in April. Arsenal's top possible score is 91, provided they defeat City.

Crucially, if Arsenal emerge victorious in that game, City's highest achievable points total drops to 86. The figures indicate that Arsenal would need to gather 19 additional points from now until the season's conclusion to claim the title.

‌



Therefore, a sum of 23 from their remaining nine games, assuming they win at City, would suffice to see them crowned champions.

The 1998 Arsenal side was fuelled by the legendary Dennis Bergkamp, who topped Arsenal's scoring charts with 22 in all competitions, alongside a robust defence led by former skipper Tony Adams. They also lifted the FA Cup that same year, overcoming Newcastle United 2-0 in the final.

View 3 Images William Saliba celebrates after scoring against Chelsea on Sunday (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Arteta's side remain active in the FA Cup, have a League Cup final approaching and are through to the last-16 of the Champions League. There exists a scenario where the Spaniard could potentially preside over Arsenal's finest campaign of all time, should they claim multiple trophies.

However, former Arsenal midfielder Nigel Winterburn reckons the 1998 title-winning squad matches any other team Wenger assembled during his 20-year tenure, including the legendary Invincible outfit of 2004.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Article continues below

Winterburn said: "In my time at Arsenal, the '98 team was the best team I played in. Luckily enough, the players we had and the players he brought in seemed to complement each other terrifically well and obviously that came to fruition very, very quickly with the 1998 Double team."

Next up for Arteta's Arsenal is a midweek journey to Brighton with the Seagulls welcoming the Gunners on Wednesday evening. City will take on Nottingham Forest at home on the same night.

Football.title: Tottenham hit with UEFA ban and fine after fans made Nazi salutes in Champions League
Football.content:
Tottenham have been fined €30,000 (£26,200) and received a suspended ban on away fans after three of their supporters were found to have made Nazi salutes. The incidents took place on January 28 during Spurs' Champions League match against Eintracht Frankfurt in Germany.

In a statement, UEFA said that the punishment was for 'the racist and/or discriminatory behaviour of its supporters'. Suspended for a year, the ban will be invoked if there is a repeat of Tottenham fans' behaviour.

The Premier League club have also been fined €2,250 (£1,965) for throwing of objects at the Deutsche Bank Park in Frankfurt. Responding to the charges, Tottenham said: "The club has been informed of sanctions handed down to us by UEFA following the utterly abhorrent conduct of a small number of individuals at our recent Champions League away match in Frankfurt.

"The club has cooperated fully with UEFA's investigation, as well as with German police on the night and, subsequently, the Met Police. We can confirm that all three individuals found to be making Nazi salutes towards Eintracht Frankfurt fans have been identified and have received indefinite bans under the Club's Sanctions and Banning Policy.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"The club stands firmly against all forms of discrimination and has therefore taken the strongest possible action. The disgusting behaviour of a minority of so-called fans on the night is in no way reflective of the values of our Club and its supporters."

Tottenham won the game 2-0 under former boss Thomas Frank, sealing automatic qualification to the last 16 of the Champions League. Spurs, now led by new manager Igor Tudor, have to drawn to face Spanish side Atletico Madrid.

The first leg will take place in Spain on Tuesday, March 10 before the return fixture in North London on Wednesday, March 18. Barcelona or Newcastle await the winners in the quarter-final stage.

BACK THE MIRROR! Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search

Tottenham's impressive form in the Champions League have been in stark contrast to their domestic struggles. Spurs are languishing 16th in the Premier League table, only five points above the relegation zone.

After losing 2-1 to Fulham on Sunday, midfielder Yves Bissouma commented: "We lost again. It is not easy, especially for us, for the club. It is not good for everyone. We know this is a big emergency. We need to change a lot of things, we need to put effort into the game to try and win games. At the moment, it is just hard."

Football.title: Todd Boehly admits Chelsea only made £63m transfer 'because Man City wanted him'
Football.content:
Todd Boehly admits Chelsea only made £63m transfer 'because Man City wanted him'

Chelsea signed Marc Cucurella during their first summer spree under Todd Boehly - with the American only targeting the defender because he knew Manchester City wanted him

View 2 Images Todd Boehly has spent over a billion pound on player transfers since taking charge at Chelsea (Image: Getty Images)

Chelsea co-owner Todd Boehly concedes he went after Marc Cucurella on the basis that "if Manchester City wants him, I want him" when he landed the defender from Brighton.

‌



The Spaniard had continued to excel with the Seagulls, back when Graham Potter was at the helm. His performances attracted the attention of some of the biggest clubs in the country with City identifying him as a target.

‌



Cucurella went as far as to put in a transfer request, such was his desire to achieve the move, but late in the day Chelsea swooped in and paid £63million to sign the defender. It was during a major summer spree, the first under Boehly's leadership.

‌



The American wasted no time in splashing the cash when he became a leading figure at Stamford Bridge and also bought in the likes of Raheem Sterling and Wesley Fofana - but admits knowing that Pep Guardiola wanted Cucurella was enough to make him sign him.

Boehly said during an event on IConnections: "So you're going to make mistakes and obviously having taken over in the circumstances that we did, basically the whole management team left. I was stuck being interim sporting director for the summer having no idea what made a good football player. But knowing that Marc Cucurella, if Man City wants him, I want him. It was really simple that way."

Forgotten Mykhalio Mudryk pops up at another English club while banned from Chelsea training READ MORE :

Chelsea handed clear VAR verdict vs Arsenal as Premier League told mistake made READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



City weren't reportedly willing to pay Brighton's asking price for the transfer, but Chelsea were ultimately prepared to cough up the cash. He has now made over 140 appearances for Chelsea and has a deal in west London until June 2028.

Cucurella initially found it difficult to make the transition from Brighton to Chelsea and his signing was not overly approved by the fanbase. It saw him criticised before eventually winning over the Blues' faithful with the defender admitting the expectations on him were huge, with his price tag a factor.

View 2 Images Marc Cucurella has won over the Chelsea fans after a difficult start

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

He said previously: "At the beginning it was very tough because the team didn't have maybe identity or didn't have a clear way to play. I struggled a little and then I understood that the club paid a lot of money for me so they expect that I'm a machine and I score every game! The first games [at Chelsea] I don't feel like I enjoyed."

Cucurella was largely a squad player until he was a starter for Spain at Euro 2024, where they would triumph. His quality was evident throughout the tournament and he's been far more appreciated since that success.

Football.title: Gary Lineker opens up on new role and reveals when TV return is set to happen
Football.content:
Gary Lineker opens up on new role and reveals when TV return is set to happen

Gary Lineker will return to our television screens in a new project the football legend is very excited about

View 2 Images Gary Lineker is excited about his new TV role (Image: Getty Images for the NTA's)

Gary Lineker has shared his excitement about appearing on his new ITV game show. The former England striker was the long-running presenter of the BBC’s flagship football programme Match of the Day between 1999 and 2025.

‌



Lineker, 65, became synonymous with the show over the course of his quarter of a century role as host, before he stepped down in an emotional farewell last season. During his time on the programme, Lineker claims he rejected a number of offers to take on other TV roles.

‌



However, it was announced in August last year that he was the host of a new ITV game show called The Box, set to air this year. The ex-Barcelona man has now revealed how much he enjoyed the show while speaking to James O’Brien on LBC’s Full Disclosure, also mentioning how it will be aired this September.

‌



Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Lineker began by jokingly saying: "I’ve got a new series… a reality show, actually, which I’m hosting. I’ve always been asked to go on reality shows and I’ve always said not until Ant & Dec retire or Claudia gives up.

"It’s called The Box and it’s a lot of fun. It’s genuinely something completely different to what I’ve done before. I liked the look of this idea. I think it’s really clever. It’s light-hearted and a lot of fun."

‌



Gary Lineker names Man Utd ace his player of the season and delivers clear red card verdict READ MORE :

BBC reveals Match of the Day viewing figures after Gary Lineker exit READ MORE :

When O’Brien highlighted how excited he sounded about another new project and challenge, Lineker admitted: "I know, amazing! I’m a bloody pensioner as well! I think they’re looking at [releasing it] very early September."

In the show, 10 yellow boxes house a celebrity contestant who will be transported to unknown locations, before being released to face whatever game awaits on the other side of the door. Each week, two players will face The Duel, a finale in which the loser leaves the competition for good.

Since leaving Match of the Day, Lineker has kept busy with his podcast, The Rest Is Football, which he hosts alongside Micah Richards and Alan Shearer.

‌



View 2 Images Lineker quit the BBC last year and left his long-standing position as host of Match of the Day (Image: PA)

Goalhanger, the podcast company Lineker co-founded and which is behind other big brands like The Rest Is Politics, The Rest is Entertainment and The Rest Is History, recently signed a deal with Netflix to turn its football edition into a daily TV show on the streaming service during this summer's World Cup, taking place across North and Central America.

Lineker had left the BBC earlier than planned due to a series of controversies surrounding his posts on social media.

Article continues below

Football.title: How many wins Arsenal and Man City need to secure Premier League title
Football.content:
How many wins Arsenal and Man City need to secure Premier League title

Arsenal have won back‑to‑back Premier League matches and lead the title race, but Manchester City remain only five points behind with a game in hand

View 2 Images The Premier League title race is set to go down to the wire (Image: PA)

Arsenal have reclaimed their position at the Premier League summit following Sunday's battling 2-1 triumph over Chelsea at the Emirates Stadium.

‌



The Gunners have now secured consecutive top-flight victories, indicating they have bounced back following their shaky period which saw them surrender points away at Brentford and Wolves, respectively. Meanwhile, Manchester City came away 1-0 winners at Leeds United with Antoine Semenyo scoring the only goal for the Citizens.

‌



As we now approach the final stretch and into the closing months of the season, the title race is made considerably more complicated by additional competitions.

‌



There remains plenty of football to be contested in the Champions League and FA Cup, alongside the Carabao Cup final between the top two.

Significantly, Arsenal have now completed one fixture more than City, who still must tackle a rescheduled home encounter against Crystal Palace in the forthcoming months.

Man City made £100m swoop and Man Utd hijacked deal as Elliot Anderson battle lines drawn READ MORE :

Mikel Arteta's squad have merely nine fixtures remaining whilst Pep Guardiola's determined challengers have 10 Premier League matches ahead.

‌



Arsenal lead the way on 64, while City are on 59. The maximum points City can reach at this juncture is 89, and that's assuming they defeat the Gunners at the Etihad Stadium.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal's maximum is 91 and that's also assuming they defeat City at the Etihad in April. Crucially, should Arsenal win that encounter, City's maximum points tally is 86. In that circumstance, the mathematics suggest Arsenal would need to collect 20 additional points from now until the campaign's conclusion to guarantee the title – which could be six wins and two draws.

‌



Therefore, a total of 23 points from the Gunners' remaining nine matches, assuming they triumph at City, would be sufficient to see them crowned champions.

View 2 Images Only five points separate Man City from Arsenal, with Pep Guardiola's side having a game in hand

City also understand that the title is still within their grasp because if they win their remaining 10 games, they will end up at the summit. If the two EFL Cup finalists were to draw in April, City would also need Arsenal to lose more points before the season's end as they would still be two points behind even if they win their game in hand.

‌



In the event of a draw when they face off in April, Arsenal would need a total of 25 points from their nine matches to mathematically clinch their first Premier League title since 2004.

FOLLOW OUR MAN CITY FB PAGE! Follow our Manchester City Mirror Football Facebook page

It's fair to say there is a long road ahead for both teams and the must-watch clash at the Etihad in April will significantly influence where the trophy ends up.

Article continues below

However, it's highly probable that the race to the finish line will go down to the wire. City host Aston Villa on the final day, while Arsenal journey across London to face Crystal Palace.

Football.title: FIFA’s Donald Trump love-in must be scrutinised as troubled 2026 World Cup looms
Football.content:
FIFA’s Donald Trump love-in must be scrutinised as troubled 2026 World Cup looms

Questions have to be asked over the FIFA president's close alignment with Donald Trump as the 100-day countdown to the tournament begins amidst America's attack on Iran

View 3 Images Gianni Infantino and Donald Trump have been very complimentary about each other in the build-up to the World Cup (Image: Tasos Katopodis - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)

At the first meeting of the so-called Board of Peace, Gianni Infantino - yep, for some reason, he is a member - donned a MAGA-style red cap emblazoned with the letters USA, and laughed. Less than two weeks later, the USA, the principal host nation of FIFA’s World Cup, bombed one of the 48 nations taking part.

‌



This is the same Infantino who recently handed a ‘FIFA Peace Prize’ to Donald Trump. If this was not so scary, it would be amusing, in an ironic way, of course.

‌



But for FIFA and every one of the associations scheduled to be represented at World Cup 2026, this is - forgive the word - deadly serious. The build-up to the past two World Cups, in Qatar and Russia, have been politically charged but this impending tournament is being clouded by unprecedented problems.

‌



Where do you start? Trump, who has promised to be a highly visible part of the World Cup, has fallen out with Canada over trade tariffs.

England Lionesses given safety assurances ahead of playing match in Turkey READ MORE :

Every country to withdraw from World Cup as Iran 'ready' to boycott USA 2026 READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

FIFA have come under fire for their ticket pricing policy. One of the host cities, Guadalajara, has been beset by brutal narco-violence four months before kick-off.

‌



And last week, the BBC reported that officials were warned there could be catastrophic consequences if the 11 US cities hosting matches do not receive funding that has been frozen amid a partial government shutdown. Now, the United States is bombing Iran, a country whose football team is scheduled to play its three Group G matches on American soil.

At the moment, FIFA clearly feels there is nothing of significance that it can say or do. But let’s face it, Infantino has already aligned himself with Trump in very emphatic fashion.

View 3 Images Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino have not been shy of taking part in pre-World Cup photo opportinities (Image: Getty Images)

‌



And whatever your views on the rights and wrongs of American foreign policy, was that really such a good idea? Having a working relationship with the president of a country that is hosting your event is clearly advisable. Fawning over him? Not so much.

Speaking at the International Football Association Board meeting in Wales, FIFA’s secretary general, Mattias Grafstrom, said they would ‘monitor developments’. He also had this very general reassurance about World Cup 2026: “Everyone will be safe.”

But that will be a question put to every national association now that geopolitical events have taken this turn, not to mention the continuing trouble in Mexico. In a couple of weeks’ time, Thomas Tuchel will name his squad for England’s friendly matches against Uruguay and Japan at Wembley at the end of the month.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

View 3 Images There were political challenges ahead of the last World Cup, in Qatar, but it went ahead with very few problems (Image: GETTY)

In his upcoming media engagements, he will find that managing the England team is not just about the football. Tuchel will be expected to have a view on the ramifications of events in Iran and the Middle East for the World Cup.

Article continues below

Right now, those ramifications do not include any threat to the tournament taking place. But they will be serious. Infantino has been slavishly happy to go along with the Make America Great Again routine from Trump. Making the World Cup great again has just got a lot harder.

Football.title: Arsene Wenger's radical offside rule change takes next step despite UEFA rejection
Football.content:
Arsene Wenger's radical offside rule change takes next step despite UEFA rejection

Former Arsenal manager Arsene Wenger has pushed for a complete overhaul of the current offside law and Ifab have now decided to greenlight an official trial of his idea

View 3 Images The change to the offside rule will be trialled in Canada (Image: Visionhaus/Getty Images)

Arsene Wenger's controversial proposed changes to the offside law have moved a step closer to becoming reality after being granted a trial in Canada. Wenger has been trying to change the offside rule since joining FIFA in November 2019 and his idea will be put to the test next month.

‌



The former Arsenal manager joined football global governing body as its chief of global football development six and a half years ago. The 76-year-old is a proponent for the 'daylight' rule being applied to offside, in order to hand the advantage back to the attacker in the hope that more goals will be scored.

‌



He has lobbied for football to go back in time and stop using technology to draw lines to determine if attackers are marginally ahead of the last defender. Instead, he wants offside to only occur if there is space between the attacker and the last defender.

‌



His radical idea has not proved popular at the International Football Association Board (Ifab), where football's lawmakers have deemed it too drastic. It has been rejected by European football's governing body, UEFA, but has now been accepted for a trial in the Canadian Premier League (CPL), which begins in April.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Premier League delete social media post after backlash for mocking Tottenham star READ MORE :

The 'daylight' rule has only previously been trialled in youth tournaments. Now Canada will become its most high-profile guinea pig, having been deemed to be at the perfect level: away from the European glare and not so elite that any blunders would be amplified.

‌



The CPL doesn't currently have the video assistant referee (VAR), but it has now potentially opened itself up for a world of controversy.

Explaining his proposal, Wenger said: "It was in 1990 after the World Cup in Italy when there were no goals scored. We decided that there is no offside any more when you are on the same line of the defender.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



View 3 Images Arsene Wenger wants to change the offside rule (Image: Getty Images)

“In case of doubt, the doubt benefits the striker. That means when there's a fraction, the striker did get the advantage. With VAR this advantage disappeared and for many people it's frustrating.

“That's why I proposed that as long as any part of your body is on the same line as the defender, you're not offside. We are experimenting with it now. In one year, the decision will be made, by the IFAB, not by me."

‌



The move comes, in part, as a reaction to the impact of semi-automated offside technology (SAOT), which has meant offside calls being judged on tiny margins. VAR is frequently taking around five minutes to rule on offside decisions at the highest level, causing huge frustration.

View 3 Images Jaidon Anthony was marginally offside in Burnley's 4-3 defeat by Brentford on Saturday (Image: @PLMatchCentre)

HAVE YOUR SAY! What do you make of Arsene Wenger's 'daylight' rule? Comment below.

‌



Just this weekend, Burnley saw a goal ruled out for an extremely tight offside against Jaidon Anthony. "I've seen the one where I'm offside. It's my shoulder, I think," Anthony said after the agonising 4-3 defeat by Brentford.

"I'm sure if I scored with that part of my body, it wouldn't have been a goal. It's disappointing."

Burnley manager Scott Parker said: "It was heartbreaking for us, really, because we deserved that. VAR and the fine margins of technology to the inch of a sleeve, calling something offside is the game of football we live in now. So we accept that. That's the way it is."

Football.title: Forgotten Mykhalio Mudryk pops up at another English club while banned from Chelsea training
Football.content:
Forgotten Mykhalio Mudryk pops up at another English club while banned from Chelsea training

Mykhailo Mudryk is serving a provisional suspension for having a banned substance in his system, with the Chelsea star sending a new message over what he's getting up to

View 4 Images Chelsea winger Mykhailo Mudryk has issued a social media update amid his ongoing doping ban (Image: (Photo by Richard Heathcote/Getty Images))

Forgotten Chelsea star Mykhailo Mudryk has shared a social media update showing him training at another English club's facilities. The 25-year-old hasn't featured for the Blues since November 2024 as he remains suspended by the FA following an 'adverse finding in a routine urine test'.

‌



The banned substance detected in his system was meldonium, which has been prohibited in sport since 2016 by the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) as it enables athletes to boost endurance, accelerate recovery, and improve oxygen delivery to tissues during exercise.

‌



Mudryk is facing a potential ban of up to four years, though the exact sanction he'll receive hasn't yet been confirmed.

‌



Chelsea handed clear VAR verdict vs Arsenal as Premier League told mistake made READ MORE :

Meanwhile, he's been working out away from Cobham with a private coach and has recently shared a video on Instagram with his 1.8 million followers showing himself in training.

The brief footage captures Mudryk on Uxbridge FC's 3G surface controlling a pass on the edge of the area before moving around an exercise dummy. He then strikes a shot into the far corner using his weaker left foot.

4 View 4 Images Mykhailo Mudryk takes the ball past an exercise dummy (Image: Mykhailo Mudryk Instagram) 4 View 4 Images Mykhailo Mudyrk takes a shot from the edge of the box (Image: Mykhailo Mudyrk Instagram)

‌



Apart from insisting he hadn't knowingly taken a banned substance, Mudryk has been relatively quiet on social media, though he did recently acknowledge a happy birthday message from Chelsea and also dropped a return hint to fans.

On the second subject, he remarked: "I just wanted to say thank you for your support. I see all your messages and truly appreciate them, so please don't give up on me as I [have] not give up on myself. Can't wait to see you soon."

Following his appointment at Chelsea to succeed Enzo Maresca, Liam Rosenior was questioned about Mudryk, with the 41-year-old acknowledging his intention to speak with the Ukrainian, though he hadn't yet done so.

‌



View 4 Images Liam Rosenior issued a Mykhailo Mudryk verdict just a few weeks into taking charge at Chelsea (Image: Offside via Getty Images)

He said in late January: "It's definitely something that I want to do, but there are many things I want to do and haven't been able to do.

"But, no, he's a magnificent player. He's had a really difficult time and when the moment is right for me and him and the club, I'll definitely have a conversation with him."

Article continues below

Rosenior further explained: "The time is right when I speak with the board and the sporting directors and when it's right that he's in a place where I can have an impact, in a positive way, on his career."

Football.title: Timber: Arsenal need to talk about and address the recent anxiety
Football.content:
Jurrien Timber has admitted Arsenal need to "address and talk about" the anxiety they are feeling when attempting to clinch victory in the closing stages of games.

The Gunners edged past Chelsea 2-1 on Sunday but needed goalkeeper David Raya to pull off a 92nd-minute save to deny Alejandro Garnacho at the death.

Since the turn of the year, Arsenal have conceded late goals against Wolves, Brentford and Manchester United which have cost them points and Timber admitted the scare against Chelsea was "unnecessary".

"You feel it, especially at the end," said Timber on Sunday evening when asked about the pressure on the team when seeing out games.

"We stopped playing a bit [against Chelsea], which was unnecessary - especially with a man up. It's something we need to work on, talk about as well.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Arsenal's match against Chelsea in the Premier League

"It happened a couple of times this season already. We put so much effort into the game, especially in the first half, and you have to go up 1-0 or 2-0, but these things happen - especially against a good team like Chelsea.

"It's part of the game, you have to understand what happens in the moment. The energy within the players and the crowd, the anxiety. It's something we need to address and talk about."

The win over Chelsea kept Arsenal five points clear of Manchester City, who have a game in hand, but Timber says the Gunners cannot start thinking the title is any closer despite there being just nine games to go for them.

"I heard it was nine games to go but it still feels so far off because we're playing in Champions League, FA Cup and Carabao Cup," he added. "Game by game. If we look too far ahead, it becomes a bit a bit too much."

'It can define a season' - Timber's praise for Raya

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player David Raya pulls off a magnificent late save to maintain Arsenal's lead against Chelsea

Timber was full of praise for Raya, who had a standout game in goal against Chelsea.

In the first half, he made a superb stop to prevent a Declan Rice own goal off the Arsenal midfielder's elbow, then made three second-half saves, including a two-handed grab to deny Joao Pedro from point-blank range. That all came before his heroics to deny Garnacho in injury time.

"David is amazing," said Timber. "Often he shows that he wins us games. He has a lot of magic moments. He's a great character and a leader in our group.

"He made a good save, [there were] some good defensive actions as well with Big Gabi [Gabriel]. These actions you need in the game, they can define a season."

Arsenal's next six fixtures - including a cup final!

March 4: Brighton (A), Premier League, kick-off 7.30pm

March 7: Mansfield Town (A), FA Cup fifth round, kick-off 12.30pm

March 11: Bayer Leverkusen (A), Champions League last 16 first leg, kick-off 5.45pm

Football.title: Riakporhe to fight TKV for British title on Fury undercard
Football.content:
Richard Riakporhe will fight Jeamie TKV for the British heavyweight title on the undercard of Tyson Fury’s comeback.

Fury returns to boxing after a year's retirement to take on Arslanbek Makhmudov at the Tottenham Hotspur stadium on April 11, with Conor Benn vs Regis Prograis the chief support.

TKV puts that British heavyweight championship he won against Frazer Clarke on the line against Riakporhe, the cruiserweight world title contender who has made a successful move up to heavyweight.

Riakporhe most recently took out Tommy Welch on the undercard of Benn's rematch with Chris Eubank Jr also at the Tottenham Hotspur stadium.

Frazer Clarke suffered a punishing defeat against TKV when they fought in November.

But the Olympic bronze medallist is going straight back into hard fight. He will box Australia's Justis Huni, who was leading his contest with Fabio Wardley last year before losing via a late knockout.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Chris Billam-Smith defended his WBO cruiserweight world title against Richard Riakporhe with a unanimous decision win at Selhurst Park

Huni said: "I'm truly honoured to be fighting in London on such a huge card headlined by Tyson Fury. The UK has a proud boxing tradition, and it means a lot to perform in front of these fans.

"April 11 is another important step in my journey, and I'm coming to deliver a strong performance and show I belong at the very top."

In addition there will be preliminary bouts: Troy Williamson vs Simon Zachenhuber; Felix Cash vs Liam O'Hare; Elliot Whale vs Tom Hill; Hector Avila Lozano vs Sultan Almohammed; Mikie Tallon vs Cristopher Rios and Breyon Gorham vs Eduard Georgiev.

Watch Terri Harper fight Caroline Dubois for the WBC and WBO world lightweight championships live on Sky Sports on Sunday April 5.

Football.title: Six Nations trophy damaged by fire, to be replaced for 2027
Football.content:
The Six Nations championship trophy has sustained fire damage and will be replaced for 2027.

After the latest round of the tournament, the vehicle transporting the trophy was caught up in an incident. No one was injured but the trophy was so damaged that it cannot be fully restored.

A new replacement will be manufactured for next year's event.

The winners of this year's tournament will be presented with an identical exhibition trophy.

The current trophy, made out of silver, was created in 2015 to "reflect the tournament's evolution".

In a statement the Six Nations said: "A new trophy will be commissioned in the same design as the original, with materials from the original being incorporated into the new trophy, ensuring its history is respectfully transferred to the new creation.

"The new trophy will take approximately 365 hours of skilled craftsmanship to create, meaning it will be unveiled ahead of the 2027 championship."

Football.title: 66 shots, one goal - Forest's goalscoring troubles to continue with Man City next?
Football.content:
Sixty-six attempts. One goal. One team with a problem to solve as the threat of relegation looms large.

Nottingham Forest's defeat to Brighton was another case of what could have been. On several occasions, boss Vitor Pereira was animated on the touchline as he watched his side spurn several good opportunities to take something from the Amex.

It is the same story that has repeated itself in recent weeks. Points have been left on the table on account of Forest's poor finishing. But is it simply poor finishing, or rather poor choices in front of goal?

It goes without saying that 66 attempts on goal across the last three games is the highest in Premier League over that period. Even leaders Arsenal, who have played a game extra in that time, don't come close. Forest also have the highest xG, standing at 4.54.

Of course, a lot of that owes to the fact that more than half of those shots came in one game. Forest mustered 35 attempts in their draw with Wolves without success. The second most with no reward in Premier League history. If just one had gone in, that would be enough for Forest to currently be sitting above Tottenham.

Image: Nottingham Forest owner Evangelos Marinakis (R) was left with his head in his hands as Forest failed to score against Wolves

But when you take a deeper dive into the numbers, something becomes apparent. Across the three games, Forest have created just 47 chances. This means close to 30 per cent of their shots at goal have come without being deemed a 'chance'.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Nottingham Forest's Premier League defeat at Brighton

Of the 66 attempts, Elliot Anderson has had the most with 12 at a combined xG of just 0.43. That's just over 0.03 per effort. Gibbs-White follows with 10 shots at a combined xG of 0.82. Incredibly, in third is defender Neco Williams with eight shots.

The two strikers that led the line in those games, Igor Jesus and Lorenzo Lucca, combined for just six attempts. It shows that Forest aren't focused on creating clear-cut opportunities for their frontmen. It's a case of quantity over quality. Especially when compared to what they produced last season.

Forest pining for Wood to return

The 47 chances created across the last three games comes to an average of 15.67 per match. That's a big increase from their season average of 9.36 and last season's figure of 8.79.

Image: Chris Wood has been out of action since October with a knee injury

The obvious difference between then and now? The absence of one Chris Wood. The New Zealander's 20-goal Premier League campaign last season was impressive enough. Add to it that his xG was just 13.35 highlights how clinical he was. Only Bryan Mbeumo outperformed his xG by a larger margin.

His xG per 90 minutes of 0.4 is comparable to Anderson's figure over the entirety of the last three. That's with Wood averaging barely over two shots on goal per game.

The only player over this current period who has remotely similar data is Lucca. His two shots registered an xG of 0.56, but he has been left on the bench ever since Vitor Pereira came in.

Lucca is also the closest mould to Wood as a player. A towering centre-forward with a physical presence in the penalty area. A target for players to aim at. That seems to suit Forest best, but if he continues to lack the efficiency, the need for Wood to return will only become more desperate.

The 34-year-old last kicked a ball in October and has been on the sidelines following knee surgery. However, Pereira has said he hopes to have the striker back soon.

It is a lot of pressure to put on someone after a lengthy spell out, but Wood will surely have little to no time to be eased back in if these problems in front of goal persist.

More of the same against City?

Before Wood makes a comeback, Pereira has to address the here and now.

A trip to the Etihad is hardly the game that most Forest fans would be wishing for during this torrid run. However, City aren't the same well-oiled machine they used to be. Teams can get at them.

City have conceded 283 shots this season - just over 10 per 90. Over that last five games though, that has risen to 13.6, indicating they are more fragile than normal.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The Super Sunday panel reflect on Gianluigi Donnarumma's critical save from Alexis Mac Allister late on, with Man City boss Pep Guardiola re-enacting the stop on the touchline

That's the positive for Forest. The negative is that it may not matter. Not only have City won all but one of those fixtures, but converting those chance into goals is proving tough for their opponents. No team has prevented more goals than Pep Guardiola's side in the Premier League this season.

Pereira has been on record saying that he will not set up his team to settle for draws. It's win at all costs. At this point, it needs to be.

But the maths is simple. If you don't score, you don't win. And Forest need to find that missing ingredient before it is too late.
